intro: Schanginia aegyptiaca, also known as Egyptian cornflower, is a plant species belonging to the family Pedaliaceae. The plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Schanginia aegyptiaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
